content:
# High Pressure High Temperature

**Summary**: Conditions where pressure exceeds 10,000 psi and temperature exceeds 300 .

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2017-alakbari|Alakbari, 2017]] evaluated drilling fluid performance under HPHT conditions.

- [[2018-basfar|BASFAR, 2018]] addressed the challenges of drilling in HPHT environments.

- [[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023]] evaluated geopolymer performance under HPHT conditions (150-194°F BHCT).

- [[2023-othman|Othman, 2023]] tested the formulation under HPHT conditions to ensure stability.

- [[2024-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2024]] evaluated the performance of OCs as rheology modifiers for OBDFs at HPHT conditions.

## Scientific Context

- [[2017-alakbari|Alakbari, 2017]] Standard conditions for deep well drilling where standard additives may fail.

- [[2018-basfar|BASFAR, 2018]] addressed the challenges of drilling in HPHT environments.

- [[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023]] evaluated geopolymer performance under HPHT conditions (150-194°F BHCT).

- [[2023-othman|Othman, 2023]] tested the formulation under HPHT conditions to ensure stability.

- [[2024-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2024]] evaluated the performance of OCs as rheology modifiers for OBDFs at HPHT conditions.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2017-alakbari|Alakbari, 2017]] Simulated using HPHT rheometers and filter presses.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Nanosilica was found to be effective for stabilizing gel strength at 200 . ([[2017-alakbari|Alakbari, 2017, p. 11]])


- The copolymer-modified fluid remained stable up to 350°F. ([[2018-basfar|BASFAR, 2018, p. 66]])

- Geopolymers showed better durability and strength at higher temperatures compared to OPC. ([[2023-abdelaal|Abdelaal, 2023, p. 38]])

- The thickening effect of the GLDA-CMHPG system was significantly reduced at temperatures reaching 100°C. ([[2023-othman|Othman, 2023, p. 82]])

- The study highlights OCs as a valuable additive for enhancing OBDF performance, particularly in challenging HPHT conditions. ([[2024-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2024, p. 16]])
